steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: A function "displays" its $y$-intercept as a constant or coefficient when that value appears explicitly and uncomputed in the written equation. Find $f(0)$ and $g(0)$, then check whether that value is visible as-is in each formula.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Function Ⅰ — $f(x)=a(3.3)^{x+b}$: $f(0)=a(3.3)^{b}$. That value is not a single constant in the equation — you must compute $(3.3)^{b}$ first. ✗
  • Function Ⅱ — $g(x)=a(3.3)^{x}+b$: $g(0)=a(3.3)^{0}+b=a+b$. The value $a+b$ is not written as one term in the equation — only the individual constants $a$ and $b$ appear, not their sum. ✗
Neither function displays its $y$-intercept directly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
